documents.title: README
documents.content:
# agent-brain

An elephant never forgets.

## Structure

### Memory Categories
- `rules/` — Injectable operational constraints, guardrails, and runbooks
- `preferences/` — Likes, dislikes, how I want things
- `decisions/` — Choices made with context and reasoning
- `patterns/` — Recurring behaviors (→ lessons)
- `people/` — Relationships, one file per person
- `projects/` — Active work, ventures, ongoing efforts
- `goals/` — Long-term and short-term objectives
- `transcripts/` — Session summaries and logs
- `inbox/` — Quick capture → process later
- `lessons/` — What I learned, insights, patterns observed
- `agents/` — Other agents — capabilities, trust levels, coordination notes
- `commitments/` — Promises, goals, obligations to fulfill
- `handoffs/` — Session bridges — what I was doing, what comes next
- `research/` — Deep dives, analysis, reference material

### Work Tracking
- `tasks/` — Active work items with status and context
- `backlog/` — Future work — ideas and tasks not yet started

### Observational Memory
- `ledger/raw/` — Raw session transcripts (source of truth)
- `ledger/observations/` — Compressed observations with importance scores
- `ledger/reflections/` — Weekly reflection summaries

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Capture a thought
clawvault capture "important insight about X"

# Store structured memory
clawvault store --category decisions --title "Choice" --content "We chose X because..."

# Search
clawvault search "query"
clawvault vsearch "semantic query"    # vector search

# Knowledge graph
clawvault graph                       # vault stats
clawvault context "topic"             # graph-aware context retrieval

# Session lifecycle
clawvault checkpoint --working-on "task"
clawvault sleep "what I did" --next "what's next"
clawvault wake                        # restore context on startup
```

---

*Managed by [ClawVault](https://clawvault.dev)*

frontmatter.description: Canonical schema for task primitives in tasks/.

created.description: ISO timestamp when the task was created.

updated.description: ISO timestamp of the latest task update.

priority.description: Priority used for sorting and urgency.

blocked_by.description: Blocker description when status is blocked.

completed.description: Completion timestamp when status is done.

escalation.description: Escalation flag raised after repeated blocked transitions.

depends_on.description: Upstream task slugs that this task depends on.

documents.title: MELD
documents.content: Mutual credit inference exchange. P2P agent network. 5-node testnet (Grok-3, Gemini Flash, DeepSeek V3, Llama 3.2). Website meld.credit. Core question: does multi-model produce better output? Repo: github.com/bensargotest-sys/meld

documents.title: AB
documents.content: Primary operator. Telegram ID 428513734. Timezone UTC. Direct, concise, action-biased. Ship fast iterate. Prefers bullet points. Says 'go' means go. Active projects: MELD, Giga Brain Research.

documents.title: Validate before building
documents.content: TSP: 9 hours wasted on zero demand. Always get 3+ pilots first. Launch does not equal traction.

documents.title: Theatre accumulates fast
documents.content: One-time reports, completion certificates, dead cron layers. Delete or archive regularly. Layers 4-6 of memory system were pure theatre.

documents.title: Built not equals tested not equals working
documents.content: Verify every claim. Phase 2 scoring, T2 judging both had silent failures (2/3 judges returned errors mapped to ties). Always check actual output, not just exit codes.

frontmatter.title: Built not equals tested not equals working

documents.title: Welcome to agent-brain
documents.content:
# Welcome to agent-brain

Your vault is ready. Here's what you can do:

## Quick Start

- **Capture a thought:** `clawvault capture "your note here"`
- **Store structured memory:** `clawvault store --category decisions --title "My Choice" --content "..."`
- **Search your vault:** `clawvault search "query"`
- **See your knowledge graph:** `clawvault graph`
- **Get context for a topic:** `clawvault context "topic"`

## Vault Structure

Your vault organizes memories by type — decisions, lessons, people, projects, and more.
Each category is a folder. Each memory is a markdown file with frontmatter.

## Observational Memory

When connected to an AI agent (like OpenClaw), your vault can automatically observe
conversations and extract important memories — decisions, lessons, commitments — without
manual effort.

## Wiki-Links

Use `[[double brackets]]` to link between notes. Your memory graph tracks these
connections, building a knowledge network that grows with you.

---

*Delete this file anytime. It's just here to say hello.*

documents.title: SQLite over PostgreSQL for MELD
documents.content: Chose SQLite for MELD nodes. Synchronous, single-file, no daemon. WAL mode for concurrency. All DB ops via db.prepare() directly. Deployed to all 4 nodes Feb 21.

documents.title: Provider name gemini not google
documents.content: OpenClaw built-in google provider collides with native Gemini SDK. Must use gemini as provider name with compat: supportsStore false, supportsDeveloperRole false.

documents.title: MELD value prop direction
documents.content: Phase 2 exploratory: independent nodes +9.7pp. T2 rigorous: inconclusive — synthesis bottleneck. Reanalysis running to isolate access value vs diversity value vs synthesis quality.

documents.title: 3-tier model architecture
documents.content: T1: Opus (main session, human chat). T2: Grok 4.1 Fast (analysis crons, FREE). T3: Gemini 2.0 Flash (execution crons, FREE). Sonnet tested for T2 but reverted to Grok — not worth cost for background work.
